explanation: This specification expresses that whenever prop_1 holds, then from that point forward (through) as long as both prop_2 and prop_3 hold, prop_0 must also hold. This uses the release operator: 'p V q' means q must hold until (and including) the moment p becomes true (or forever if p never becomes true). Here, as long as prop_2 and prop_3 are true (they 'hold through'), prop_0 must also be true. And this initiates whenever prop_1 holds.

This requirement states that the patient must never perform (shall not) prop_0 during any period when the conditions prop_1, prop_2, and prop_3 all hold simultaneously. In LTL, this is formalized as: globally, if prop_1, prop_2, and prop_3 are all true, then prop_0 must not be true at that moment.

The requirement states that whenever prop_1, prop_2, and prop_3 are all true simultaneously, it must not be the case that the patient is connected to the breathing circuit (which is represented by prop_0). In LTL, this is best expressed as a global property: at all times, it is not allowed that prop_0 is true while all of prop_1, prop_2, and prop_3 are also true.

The requirement expresses that the patient must not do prop_0 when prop_1 holds and from that point until prop_3 holds, prop_2 holds. In LTL, 'through' is understood as 'from now until prop_3, prop_2 holds'. Thus, whenever prop_1 holds and prop_2 holds continuously until prop_3, prop_0 must not hold at that time.

The statement says that 'prop_0' holds whenever both 'prop_1' and 'prop_2' and 'prop_3' hold. In LTL, this is typically translated as: globally, if 'prop_1' and 'prop_2' and 'prop_3' are all true, then 'prop_0' is true. This can be expressed as G((prop_1 & prop_2 & prop_3) -> prop_0).

The sentence indicates that prop_0 should hold whenever both prop_1, prop_2, and prop_3 all hold. In LTL, this is best expressed as: globally, if (prop_1 and prop_2 and prop_3) hold, then prop_0 also holds.

This requirement states that 'prop_0' should not hold whenever 'prop_1', 'prop_2', and 'prop_3' are all true at the same time. Thus, globally, if all of 'prop_1', 'prop_2', and 'prop_3' are true, then 'prop_0' must be false.

The formula captures that whenever both prop_1 holds, and throughout the holding of both prop_2 and prop_3, then prop_0 also holds. In LTL, this is expressed with an implication guarded by the global operator for the condition 'prop_1 & prop_2 & prop_3'.

This statement means that whenever prop_1 becomes true, then prop_0 must hold continuously as long as prop_2 and prop_3 remain true ('through' prop_2 and prop_3). This can be captured by stating that whenever prop_1 holds, prop_0 must hold at least until either prop_2 or prop_3 become false.

The requirement states that the patient must not exhibit 'prop_0' whenever both 'prop_1' is true and continuously from that moment through 'prop_2' and 'prop_3'. This means that from any point when 'prop_1' becomes true, and as long as 'prop_2' and 'prop_3' remain true, 'prop_0' must always be false. In LTL, we can write this as: globally, if 'prop_1' is true, then as long as 'prop_2' and 'prop_3' hold, 'prop_0' is always false. This can be captured as: G(prop_1 -> (!prop_0 U !(prop_2 & prop_3))).
